title: Docs: Explicitly compare --mount with alternative flags (-v, --tmpfs)
body:
The documentation does not explicitly compare the differences between using `--mount` and `-v` (and `--mount` vs `--tmpfs`) where there are overlaps in use cases. In particular, there should be explicit comparisons for:

- `-v <host-dir>:<...>` vs `--mount=type=bind,src=<host-dir>,<...>`
- `-v <source-volume>:<...>` vs `--mount=type=volume,src=<source-volume>,<...>`
- `--tmpfs` vs `--mount=type=tmpfs`

My understanding is that `-v` with a `host-dir` is (roughly?) the same as `--mount=type=bind` but with a different syntax for the options (with both not sharing all options), and similarly for the other two pairs.

Is the above correct? In any case, I believe that the answers should be documented explicitly in the `--mount`, `--volume`, and `--tmpfs` sections.

Additionally, it would be nice if the documentation stated which one is recommended to use over the others in cases where they overlap. FWIW, Docker recommends [here](https://docs.docker.com/engine/storage/bind-mounts/#syntax) and [here](https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/container/run/#mount) that they recommend `--mount` over `-v`, and [here](https://github.com/docker/cli/blob/fc99fe2d08307b947de2580a0d46779da2b235d0/man/docker-run.1.md?plain=1#L707) for `--mount` over `--tmpfs`; of course the recommendations and reasons may be different for Podman.

comments.body: I would like to take this issue. I am using Podman as a docker alternative at my workplace, and would like to gain extensive knowledge of the product overall
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
